response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer, but the role demands native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, and Linux kernel source-modification experience — none of which are evidenced. Their C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (2014–2015), and the bulk of their career centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms rather than embedded networking, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), or switch firmware. The hard language requirement alone is disqualifying.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, and some systems work
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience (DLang, async I/O) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong testing culture and craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling)
concerns: No indication of Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement (native level)
Minimal recent C/UNIX experience and no Linux kernel investigation or modification background
No embedded networking or security-domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, SNMP/NETCONF)

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded C / Linux kernel experience
General seniority and systems programming exposure
